message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 2 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: Finding math/thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible/setup requires an orchestrator-side statement change, not an in-place core edit. The theorem statement currently quantifies bidirected-disconnection against every ancestor of P_v union {v}, which includes i_r itself because the note uses inclusive ancestry (cf. An(i_r)={i_r}); the proof and supporting lemma instead use the distinct-ancestor version. Repairing this mismatch would change the theorem's asserted premise, which the hard limit assigns to the proto/orchestrator rather than D0.R. The decision findings on thm:closed-form-coefficient/novelty-floor-miss and thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible/subclass-positioning are prose-positioning items, but this round must escalate because not every finding can be discharged without first fixing that statement-level defect.
Open findings: setup@thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible, novelty-floor-miss@thm:closed-form-coefficient, subclass-positioning@thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible. Provide guidance via the D0 directive (a new direction / a paper to adapt / a reframing) and re-run, or rewind D0/D-1.2.
next_step_guidance:
⚙ AUTO MODE — make this decision YOURSELF per the rules below and `--resume` WITHOUT asking the user (do the substrate-build / literature / maximality / de-launder work autonomously). Stop and report to the user ONLY on a terminal failure (math claim wrong, proven-intractable, a cap/budget block) — otherwise keep driving to CKPT 2.

D0.5 halt — READ the halt message body + `stage_completed` to determine the ACTUAL outcome before acting: a genuine PASS has stage_completed="0.5" and reviews/review_general.json present (→ D0.5→F1 go/no-go); a non-converging or below-floor halt keeps stage_completed="0" and did NOT pass (→ fix findings / lift tier and re-run D0.5). Never assume PASS from the stage alone. SKILL §'Watch keys' D0.5 row.
